title: Where my lipomas came from - Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about the origin of his lipomas, theorizing they come from environmental toxins that mutate fat cells, drawing parallels to cancer. He shares personal experience having them removed and biopsied, then extends into a wide-ranging monologue about cleaning up the environment through aeroponic skyscrapers, eliminating pesticides and pharmaceuticals in water, ending oil imports, and transitioning to electric vehicles as ways to reduce cancer and lipomas.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: caused by environmental toxins mutating fat cells, similar to how cancer starts
rising due to both longer lifespans and increased toxin exposure; predicts half of people will get it
major pollutant that should be eliminated and replaced with sealed aeroponic skyscrapers
contaminate water systems and should be stopped to reduce chemical exposure
should stop importing from the Middle East and repurpose oil only for building materials like plastic skyscrapers
causes pollution and cancer, should be stopped
hermetically sealed aeroponic skyscrapers in every town providing free food, humanity will be vegetarian
important to get annual screenings; Medicaid access matters for this
can be done with ice and squeezing; has shown it on YouTube

transcript.text: All right, here's a video request from Rod Stottinger, the fisherman from New South Wales.
Oops, I shouldn't eat one. I'm doing a video.
He wanted to know where do I think lipomas come from and how do they start.
Is it like a diet or is it something like this or something like that, like maybe dirty?
Okay, lipomas I think come from the toxins in the environment.
I think that subcutaneous fatty lipomas, which is what I have, is these lumps that I get on my body and I have a bunch now.
They started growing when I was like 35 or something, I don't know, and then they just all started like, yeah.
Oh, here's this. I've been looking for this. That's amazing.
Yeah, so I think I look at lipomas as a form of cancer, and cancer I think is caused by toxins in the environment.
I read a lot of history books, you know, and in history a lot of people just die.
They suddenly just die or they get sick and they die.
And so I think cancer has always existed.
And so whenever somebody here is not somebody who just gets sick and die, I think that probably has been cancer, you know.
For example, you know, Queen Bloody Mary died of ovarian, of uterian cancer.
And they thought she was pregnant for a long time for that now.
But I think the cancer rate is going up a lot, partly, I guess, naturally because people are living longer, so it gives you more time to get cancer.
But also, percentage-wise, even for the young ages, people who are getting cancer, I think, are on the rise.
I think it's predicted that half the people are going to get cancer.
My mom's had cancer twice, and my dad had cancer twice, and I did.
And, yeah, so I think it's just from these toxins, you know.
Like, for example, how chemotherapy, how x-rays can give you cancer, you know.
They send a little x-ray, and it goes in, and it tweaks with your DNA.
It hits your DNA because it's so small.
And then your DNA mutates, and then it spreads, mutates a cell, and then it gets out of control.
And I think toxins can do that, too, because if you have toxins like lead, something really small or bad can get inside of the spine tissues of your body between the cells,
And then it becomes like, you know, they have to become encapsulated, a benign tumor.
Well, you know, like if you forget to take your stitches out, and then your skin would just grow over it and then encapsulate, it becomes like a benign tumor.
But if you have all these little benign tumors all over your body, and these tiny little chemicals floating around, you know, zillions of them,
Like the chances of one becoming, hitting the DNA or becoming mutated and mutating the cells that just mutate and turn into cancer, you know,
That's going to increase that, all the different toxins and chemicals in your body is going to increase that.
And so I think what I developed is just like a, I'm kind of like scratching the surface of cancer, you know.
Like whenever I got my lipoma out of my back and they sent it in for biopsy, and I took the staph infection out of my neck and they sent it in for biopsy,
And then they referred me to a dermatologist to scoop it all out to make sure it's all out so it doesn't get cancerous, skin cancerous.
So, yeah, and I got blood tests done, but I think it's good to come in like every year and, you know, get the cancer screenings.
Because that's one good reason why you need to have Medicaid to get people in there for this cancer screenings early so that they don't find out too late and die of cancer.
You know, that's how, I think that's how my dad died because he found out about his pancreatic cancer too late.
So, you know, and so, yeah, but I think, you know, I think that this kind of lipoma thing has always happened.
It's probably, you know, it's some kind of mutation of the fat cell.
The fat cells just mutate, and then they grow, you know, and it's not going to stop.
It just grows.
But luckily you can cut these out.
So, but in the past, I don't think that, I think people have always gotten these in the past, and I think that they could always deal with it.
Even if it was in caveman times, if we were in tribal times, and I had this lipoma, I would still cut it out.
I wouldn't have ice.
Well, I guess I would have ice.
I'd just be like, yeah, yeah, wait until the wintertime, and then you hold some of your ice snowball on top of it, and then we'll get a piece of obsidian.
Because literally, like, if I was stuck in the middle of nowhere, I could take my light bones out, like, without an eye.
But all I would do is I'd go find an obsidian, I would hold a snowball over it for a half hour, I'd pinch it out, basically make a gash with the obsidian, squeeze, and it's done, you know?
So, you know, that's super easy.
I've shown it on YouTube.
Maybe I'll have a link below of them.
There's even another video, I don't know if it's still up, if it's my friend who cut it out and there's a giant one on my leg.
I'll put that on, if I can put it up there, if I can find it.
She may have deleted it.
I never saved it, so.
But I have some other lipoma videos on here.
So that's where I think they come from.
I'm glad that if I have a physical problem, that's the one, because it's easier to deal with.
And I like poppy because it's in there.
But I think in the future, we'll really need to focus on how it's having a clean environment.
You can have a clean environment if you stop using pesticides.
Pesticides gets into the water system, and all the chemicals stop using pharmaceutical drugs because that gets in the water system as well,
Which then we have to recirculate.
And then when you recirculate it from the water filters or the wells and stuff, you don't always circulate all of it out,
Especially when it's with chemicals.
I mean, you could be ingesting chemicals in the water that was somebody else's drug or whatever.
So just try to keep the water as clean as you possibly can by stop taking all the pharmaceutical drugs and just smoking weed
And then stop using pesticides.
Those are really bad because it's, I mean, imagine if we used pesticides 1,000 years.
1,000 years to drop down any pesticides in there.
So stop using pesticides and just replace them with aeroponic skyscrapers in the middle of every town
That are hermetically sealed so bugs can't get in and out, so you don't have to use any pesticides.
You can grow whatever you want.
And then you can save the phosphorus that way.
You recycle the phosphorus much easily if you have it within the cryogenically sealed container.
And then you just send it out on these, like, tubes throughout the city that just dump at regular intervals,
The very simple produce, the plants and the veg, because we'll all be vegetarian in the future
And we'll all be eating at a predictable and regular rate.
And it could all be automated, so we could all get free food when we'd have to work.
And they'd be clean.
No pesticides and no ground.
The ground wouldn't be getting polluted at all because stuff would be growing in the skyscrapers
And you could basically turn the world into a garden again.
And that would get rid of the lipomas and all the cancers, I think,
Is by just cleaning up the environment, you know,
Because then we won't have petrol cars anymore.
It would all be electric cars and we'll be using our petrol to make buildings,
To make these skyscrapers, you know, big Lego skyscrapers built out of refined oil
Or the oil taken into plastic and all that.
So, you know, so we should jack up the oil prices.
What we need to do is stop importing oil from the Middle East
And just let those guys be simple again because if they get too powerful,
They could get a nuclear bomb and attack everybody because they're fucking crazy.
They're like monkey people running around with a bunch of money.
At least now they are.
But, see, all we have to do is wait.
If we can ward off destroying the world for another 50 years,
Our kids will be smart enough to value –
Everybody in the world will be smart enough to value all that good stuff.
But, yeah, we should stop importing oil from there.
We should stop fracking because that will pollute the groundwater
And give everybody cancer.
As you can already see what's happening in – what was it?
Flint, Michigan?
No, yeah, and like in Oklahoma and stuff.
And, you know, people are having to sue and all that from retarded kids and all that.
So, yeah, and then just jack up the oil prices sky high so that we can deter all of those kinds of vehicles.
And then just use the oil to build – or, I don't know, keep the prices low so that you can keep pumping it.
But just the oil that are already in – and the oil wells already have.
But only use that oil to build – to build our buildings, all of our skyscrapers with – our plastic skyscrapers.
That'll last like a thousand years, you know.
So, that's that.
That's what – that's where the lipomas came from, I think.
Okay.
Peace out.
